Contribute to a premier economics department and shape future economic leaders.


Apply now to join a globally recognised economics department and drive cutting-edge research in econometrics.


Investing in you - benefits package including salary packaging, health and wellbeing programs, discounted services, and professional development opportunities.

The University of Melbourne
We take pride in our people, who all contribute to our mission to benefit society through the transformative impact of education and research. Discover more via our website.

Your next career opportunity
As a Senior Lecturer or Associate Professor in Econometrics at the University of Melbourne, you'll join one of Australia's premier economics departments. You'll have the opportunity to conduct influential research, teach at the forefront of econometrics, and shape the next generation of economic thinkers. Your expertise will contribute to our department's global reputation and impact economic policy and practice.

Your responsibilities will include:

Conduct High-Impact Research: Publish in top-tier journals, secure competitive grants, and engage with international academic networks to advance econometric knowledge.


Deliver Excellence in Teaching: Develop and teach specialist courses in econometrics at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, fostering student success and satisfaction.


Provide Academic Leadership: Mentor junior colleagues, contribute to departmental initiatives, and represent the university in professional forums.


Supervise Research Students: Guide Honours, Masters, and PhD students, creating a rigorous and supportive research environment.

You may be a great fit if:
You are an accomplished econometrician with a strong track record of publications in leading journals, a passion for teaching excellence, and the ability to secure competitive research funding and contribute to the department's international standing

You may also:

Have a PhD in Economics or Econometrics with a focus on advanced quantitative methods.


Possess a robust publication record in top-tier economics and econometrics journals.


Demonstrate experience in securing competitive research grants and supervising postgraduate students.


Have proven teaching excellence at undergraduate and postgraduate levels in econometrics and quantitative methods.


Show a commitment to academic leadership and the ability to contribute to the department's strategic goals.

For further information please refer to the attached PD.

What we offer you!
We offer the opportunity to be part of a vibrant community and enjoy a comprehensive range of benefits to support your success and sense of fulfillment, including:

Supportive flexible work arrangements underpinned by our commitment to inclusion and well-being


Progressive, considerate leave provisions to empower your work-life balance


Salary packaging and access to a range of discounted services including Bupa health insurance


Health and well-being services including a leading Employee Assistance Program

For more information check out our benefits page!

Your new team - Department of Economics
The Department of Economics at the University of Melbourne is a leading economics department in Australia. It has 95 academic staff and is part of a larger community of economists at the university. This includes the Melbourne Business School with 11 academic economists and econometricians and the Melbourne Institute of Applied Economic and Social Research with 43 researchers undertaking research and consultancy projects for government and private sector clients.

The Faculty of Business and Economics holds Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) accreditation and European Quality Improvement System (EQUIS) accreditation. It hosts world class academics and professional staff, the brightest students, alumni, and other stakeholders. We are committed to enhancing our applied research outcomes, expanding our curriculum, and bolstering our connections with industry to help shape the world of business and economics in Australia and internationally.

Struber is a national, human‑based services consultancy. We work across the public and private sectors – particularly infrastructure and service‑delivery environments – helping organisations engage meaningfully, communicate clearly, and manage change in ways that improve experiences for people and communities.

We are values‑led, outcomes‑focused, and proud of a culture that balances pace, clarity, energy and commercial discipline.




About the Role

As a Senior Consultant, you will play a hands‑on role in delivering people‑centered work while helping shape how we grow in Victoria.




You will:




Deliver and lead meaningful work

Lead and deliver people‑focused projects across engagement, communication and change

Work confidently in complex, fast‑moving or start‑up environments

Bring structure, rigour and momentum to ambiguous problems

Drive clarity and change

Design and implement communication, engagement and change approaches

Build trusted relationships

Partner closely with clients and stakeholders, from community members to senior executives

Represent Struber with confidence and professionalism in client environments

Contribute to culture and capability

Coach and support consultants and emerging talent

Role‑model Struber’s Bright Mantras: pace, clarity, energy and commercial discipline

Actively contribute to a positive, inclusive and high‑performance culture

Help shape how we work as the Victorian practice grows

Are comfortable working in ambiguity and change

Balance human‑centered thinking with commercial awareness and delivery discipline

Bids, tenders, proposals or submissions

Project management in people‑focused environments




You might come from:

Communications, engagement or change teams

Social impact or community‑focused organisations

Project manage high-impact Saas implementations for Government clients.

About the Role


Insight Data Solutions is a business within a business - using technology to drive efficiency and transparency around land valuations, leveraging new digital capabilities that will minimise bottlenecks and create process efficiencies in delivering valuations for Government entities e.g. council owned properties and land.

Through our unique mix of robust technology and comprehensive industry expertise, IDS enables governments, corporates and consumers to meet their property valuation needs.


As a Project Manager, you'll play a key role in the improvement of Government services through innovation, as we create the national property platform for Government. Working with Product & Engineering teams, you'll drive the successful implementation of VM Online - our Computer Assisted Mass Appraisal (CAMA) software, to Victorian Government from planning to delivery, and support an ongoing roadmap of upgrades and releases with the help of project and support teams.


12 month contract.

Put simply, our Data Solutions division is a true market leader! This is a unique opportunity to join a unique "business within a business", in a very supportive and close-knit culture with a huge pipeline of interesting and varied projects on the horizon to look forward to.


In a typical day you can expect to:


Lead end-to-end delivery of our SaaS implementation projects for government clients - owning scope, timelines, budget and quality while keeping everyone aligned and informed.

Partner closely with Product, Engineering and PMO teams to deliver high-impact outcomes using our hybrid Agile and Waterfall delivery practices.

Act as a trusted client-facing advisor - building strong stakeholder relationships and ensuring each project delivers real business value and ROI.

Drive governance and reporting frameworks that give clarity and confidence to our PMO and Executive teams.

Identify, manage and mitigate risks, issues and change requests - keeping projects moving smoothly and transparently.

Track project performance, financials and success metrics - documenting milestones and ensuring benefits are realised in line with the business case.

Plan and coordinate release activities - managing cross-team dependencies, resolving challenges and communicating release impacts clearly.

Contribute to a high-performing, collaborative team culture - embedding best practice, encouraging engagement and helping shape the future of government property technology.


Why Join Us:


We're the right-sized business for you to make a real impact, with a workplace culture where you can be you. There's so many perks! Here's some of the favourites…


Hybrid working between home, office and visiting clients.
Wellbeing Leave.
Leadership development programs, LinkedIn Learning and more.

Regular social events including our famous Innovation Days and annual BBQ!



Our Ideal Person:


ESSENTIAL:


A Bachelors degree or equivalent from an accredited university or college.

Proficient in Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC).

Experience delivering to Government or large corporate clients.

Experiencing working closely with Product and Technical teams.

Experience with Waterfall, Agile and Lean methodologies.

Superior communication and relationship management skills.

Highly productive, with the ability to lead and work both independently and as a team member.
